description.value: **Benefit:** When you brew a potion, you can have the potionâs effects be delayed by up to 10 rounds after a creature consumes it. Once you decide how many rounds the potionâs effects will be delayed by, that decision cannot later be changed. When a delayed potion is imbibed, it has no effect for the chosen number of rounds, and then it affects the imbiber as normal. The rounds in which the potionâs effects are delayed do not count against the duration of the potionâs effect. A spell brewed into a potion this way does not use up a higher-level spell slot than the spellâs actual level.
